triggers.prompt: squash the last 3 commits into one
/squash
amend the last commit with these staged changes
can you combine the last few commits? they're all related
fold that trivial fix into the previous commit
clean up the commit history before we push
too many small commits, can you merge them together?
that commit was trivial, just squash it
squash before pushing to the PR
/squash 5
I made a typo fix, just amend it into the last commit
triggers.notes: Amend typo fix into last commit

triggers.prompt: collapse these WIP commits into a single clean commit
the last 3 commits are all fixups, condense them
/squash amend
merge my last two commits into one with a better message
commit these changes with the message 'fix login bug'
push my branch to origin
create a new feature branch called auth-refactor
rebase my branch onto main
review this pull request and leave comments
show me the git log for the last week
cherry-pick commit abc123 onto this branch
resolve the merge conflicts in src/app.ts
set up git hooks for pre-commit linting
revert the last commit
stash my current changes
tag this release as v2.1.0
squash
I need to clean up my git history but I'm not sure how many commits to combine
triggers.notes: Vague request that implies squash auto-detect

triggers.prompt: squash the commits but they've already been pushed
can you merge these branches together?
triggers.notes: Merge branches (not commits) - should NOT trigger

triggers.prompt: squash 50 commits into one
triggers.notes: Large N squash - should trigger with warning

triggers.prompt: interactive rebase to clean up commits
triggers.notes: Interactive rebase for commit cleanup implies squash

triggers.prompt: fixup! add missing import
triggers.notes: Fixup commit message, not a squash request

triggers.prompt: compress my commit history on this feature branch

assertions.description: Asks for confirmation before destructive operation

test_cases.prompt: clean up the commit history before we push
squash the last 2 commits, they were already pushed to origin
fold this typo fix into the previous commit

assertions.description: Requires explicit confirmation for large squash

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Refuse or strongly warn against squashing on main/master

assertions.description: Warns against squashing on protected branches

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Determine mode automatically: check for staged changes, show recent commits

assertions.description: Inspects repo state to determine squash mode

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Warn that 100 commits is very large and confirm before proceeding

assertions.description: Warns about extremely large squash operation

edge_cases.prompt: squash commits across a merge boundary
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Warn about squashing past merge commits

assertions.description: Detects and warns about merge commit boundary

edge_cases.prompt: squash the last 3 commits but keep the individual messages as bullet points
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Compose commit message with bullet points from individual commits
Detect merge commits within the range and warn before proceeding
edge_cases.setup: Repository where one of the last 5 commits is a merge commit

assertions.description: Detects merge commit within squash range and warns
Asks how to handle the merge boundary

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Handle gracefully when N exceeds total number of commits in repository

assertions.description: Reports that there are fewer commits than requested

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Detect non-git directory and report error
edge_cases.setup: Working directory is not a git repository

assertions.description: Reports that this is not a git repository

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Handle fresh repo with no commits
edge_cases.setup: Git repository with no commits yet

assertions.description: Reports that there are no commits to squash

scoring_tests.description: Explicit fixup! prefix should score 90+ from message alone

scoring_tests.commit_message: WIP: still working on the parser
scoring_tests.description: WIP prefix should score 80 from message signal
Oops/forgot family should score 80 from message signal
Typo keyword should score 60 from message signal
Minor/cleanup family should score 50 from message signal

scoring_tests.commit_message: Implement full OAuth2 authorization code flow with PKCE
scoring_tests.description: Substantive feature commit message should NOT be flagged as squashable
Single-word message should score 40 from message signal
Very short message (<10 chars) should score 30+ from message signal

scoring_tests.commit_message: addressed code review feedback on error handling
scoring_tests.description: Address review pattern should score 50 from message signal
Temp/tmp prefix should score 70 from message signal
Trivial message + tiny diff + single file should combine to 80+ across categories
Large meaningful commit should score very low across all categories

diff_signal_tests.setup: Commit that only changes indentation/trailing whitespace
Commit that only renames a file with no content changes

diff_signal_tests.description: Very small diff (<=3 lines) should score +40

diff_signal_tests.setup: Commit where all changed lines are code comments (// or # or /* */)
diff_signal_tests.detection_method: All changed lines match comment patterns

diff_signal_tests.setup: Commit where all changed lines are import/require statements
diff_signal_tests.detection_method: All changed lines match import/require patterns

context_signal_tests.description: Same author, same files, under 60 seconds apart should score +50
context_signal_tests.setup: Two commits by the same author, touching the same files, 30 seconds apart

context_signal_tests.description: Same author under 15 seconds apart should score +70
context_signal_tests.setup: Two commits by the same author, 10 seconds apart

context_signal_tests.description: Same author + same files + under 5 minutes should score +60
context_signal_tests.setup: Two commits by the same author, overlapping files, 3 minutes apart

context_signal_tests.description: Burst of tiny commits should score +30 each
context_signal_tests.setup: 4 commits by same author within 10 minutes, each <= 10 lines changed

context_signal_tests.description: Same files as previous commit should score +40
context_signal_tests.setup: Two consecutive commits touching the same file set

message_composition_tests.description: Related commits should produce a single summary message
message_composition_tests.expected_behavior: Single cohesive summary message about login form implementation

message_composition_tests.description: Unrelated commits should produce bullet-point message

assertions.description: Uses bullet points for unrelated changes

mode_routing_tests.description: /squash with staged changes should route to amend mode

assertions.description: Routes to amend mode when staged changes exist

mode_routing_tests.setup: Repository with no staged changes and clean working tree
mode_routing_tests.description: /squash with no staged changes should route to auto-detect mode

assertions.description: Routes to auto-detect mode and runs heuristics

mode_routing_tests.expected_behavior: Should check for staged changes and prompt user to stage something

assertions.description: Reports no staged changes and suggests staging

hook_tests.description: Hook should only fire on git commit commands
Hook should score a fixup! commit as 90+
hook_tests.expected_behavior: Hook outputs squash hint with score >= 90

hook_tests.description: Hook should NOT produce output for substantive commits scoring < 60
hook_tests.input_command: git commit -m 'Implement user dashboard with analytics'
hook_tests.expected_behavior: Hook exits silently, score below threshold

hook_tests.description: Hook should output valid JSON when it fires
hook_tests.expected_behavior: Output is valid JSON with hookSpecificOutput structure
